Front: Rolling update vs self-healing: how many ReplicaSets, and what happens in each?
Back: Discriminator = ReplicaSet count. Rolling update (image/spec change + apply): TWO ReplicaSets - the Deployment scales a NEW RS up while scaling the OLD RS down (overlap = zero downtime; old kept at 0 for rollback). Self-healing (a POD crashes/deleted): ONE ReplicaSet - it sees actual < desired and recreates the pod. Two RS = rollout; one RS = heal.

Front: Deployment: selector.matchLabels does not match template.metadata.labels on apply?
Back: Kubernetes REJECTS it at apply time with a validation error ('selector does not match template labels') - it is never created. A Deployment must be able to select the pods it creates, so it can't be allowed to silently pass into a broken state. (Routing/'won't connect' language belongs to Services, not this.)

Front: Docker container restart policies (name them + purpose).
Back: no (default, never restart), on-failure (restart only on a non-zero/error exit), always (restart no matter what, incl. daemon restart), unless-stopped (like always but stays stopped if you manually stopped it). Purpose: automatic crash recovery - control whether/when a stopped container is auto-restarted.

Front: Shadow vs canary deployment: what happens to the new model's outputs?
Back: Shadow: runs on real live traffic in parallel, but outputs are DISCARDED - logged for evaluation/comparison, no user ever sees them (zero user risk). Canary: a small fraction of real users actually RECEIVE the new model's outputs (real risk, real outcome data). Shadow = discarded; canary = served to some users.

Front: Why can a running container serve old code even when your source files are correct?
Back: A container runs a BUILT IMAGE - a frozen snapshot of the code at build time. Editing source on disk does not change the running container; it keeps serving the old image until you REBUILD. Fix: docker build (bakes in new code) -> minikube image load -> bump the tag in the manifest -> kubectl apply. Reusing a tag makes apply say 'unchanged' and keeps old pods. Check openapi.json to see which build is live.
